text: A brand new medical treatment seems to have saved this little boy's life, and it could save a lot more kids like him out of the 20,000 genes in his body. One has a rare mutation that makes it impossible for him to produce an important protein, causing a life-threatening disorder that can severely damage his organ's overtime. So, scientists started taking stem cells from his blood, and then, using a harmless virus as a messenger, they inserted a new working gene. And when they put the stem cells back into his body, it seems to be working. The edited stem cells multiplied and started producing the missing protein inside his body. And the best part is, he's the first of five children around the world who will receive this treatment. Maybe many more. If you need more optimistic science and text stories, follow along.
